next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 2

North Stacks C-13 did not exist in any arrangement the library still admitted to using.

Owen proved that to himself three different ways before supper and twice after it, because there was always the chance that he had made one small stupid transcription error and built the whole thing out of that. He checked the current shelf index by hand until Madam Pince told him to stop breathing on it. He whispered the location charm fourth-years were taught for lost essays and watched the spell shiver uselessly over every respectable part of the north wall except the blasted section, where it went dull and dropped like wet thread. He measured the exposed stone numerals again, fingers in the cracks left by Amycus's last fit of disciplinary enthusiasm, and found the same thing he had found the night before: the modern shelving stood over an older system whose numbers had been cut straight into the stone, and C-13 belonged to that older system, not this one.

By nine o'clock the library had that late, watchful quiet it got under occupation. Nothing about it was peaceful; the room felt emptied in the wrong way. A room could keep hundreds of books and still feel as if it had been searched for valuables. The hanging lamps were turned low. Dust sat in the broken joints of the north stacks where blasting had shifted the cases half an inch off true. Owen was supposed to be checking spines for damage on the nearest trolley. He had checked six. The rest sat untouched while he crouched in the gap between two leaning cases with the old Binns copy open on his knee and a second book b...
open_hooks_to_carry: Establish Hogwarts under Snape and the Carrows as a place of daily humiliation rather than heroic set piece.
Place Owen at the Hufflepuff breakfast table as competent at getting by and evasive about helping.
state_deltas_required: Occupied-school routine, Martha's push toward open resistance, and Owen's desire to stay unnoticed while the library damage offers an opening.
Owen moves from passive endurance to active secret pursuit once the footnote and obsolete shelf marks line up.
